info.name: Hoverworks Tests
info.description:
An API for testing a mock storefront's catalogue of items and orders.

**Author:** Jared Eller

**Class:** SDEV 372- Cloud Application Deployment

**Quarter:** Fall 2024

Endpoints:

- /items: accesses items in the storefront's catalogue
    
- /orders: accesses orders "customers" have placed for items in the catalogue

item.name: Items

request.description:
Returns all items in the storefront's catalogue.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - All items returned successfully.

Accepts a numeric ID as a path variable and returns the item associated with that ID.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - Item successfully returned.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - Provided ID was not a number.
    
- 404 (Not Found) - Provided ID does not exist in the database.

body.raw:
{
            "prodType": "Hoverskates",
            "prodPrice": 20000.99,
            "prodName": "LV Lucullian",
            "prodDesc": "Hoverworks' limited-edition collaboration with world-renowned fashion brand Louis Vuitton is live! Featuring a luxurious, elegant design on the boot and a specially-made engine to ensure a seamless, soundless transition from galesurfing to walking. Money talks, but wealth whispers.",
            "stock": 30,
            "deckLength": null,
            "skateSize": 8
}

request.description:
Accepts user-submitted parameters and creates a new database entry with them.

Responses:

- 201 (Created) - Entry creation successful.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - Data was not formatted correctly.
    

Here is an example request that follows proper format:

body.raw:
{
            "prodType": "Hoverskates",
            "prodPrice": 75,
            "prodName": "EC0-CLA55 V.1",
            "prodDesc": "For the economically-conscious worker, introducing Hoverworks' latest line of hoverskates! You spend most of the day running around for a boss that really just doesn't appreciate you enough. Why not give your feet a break and hover above the ground once you've clocked out? You can't make the post-modern world go round if it feels like you're stepping on hot coals every time you take a step, after all!",
            "stock": null,
            "deckLength": null,
            "skateSize": 13
}

request.description:
Accepts parameters and a numeric ID from the user and updates a database entry with them.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - Database record was updated successfully.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - Parameters did not follow proper format.
    
- 404 (Not Found) - No entry in the database exists with the provided ID.
    

Here is an example of a properly-formatted request:

Deletes an item with a user-provided ID from the database.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - Item has been deleted from the database successfully.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - ID was not numeric.
    
- 404 (Not Found) - No record was found in the database with the provided ID.

item.description: Various HTTP requests to interact with the various items offered by the storefront.

item.name: Orders

request.description:
Returns all orders in the database.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - All orders returned successfully.

Accepts a numeric ID as a path variable and returns the order associated with that ID.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - Order successfully returned.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - Provided ID was not a number.
    
- 404 (Not Found) - Provided ID does not exist in the database.

Accepts user-submitted parameters and creates a new database entry with them.

Responses:

- 201 (Created) - Entry creation successful.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - Data was not formatted correctly.
    

Here is an example request that follows proper format:

Accepts parameters and a numeric ID from the user and updates a database entry with them.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - Database record was updated successfully.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - Parameters did not follow proper format.
    
- 404 (Not Found) - No entry in the database exists with the provided ID.
    

Here is an example of a properly-formatted request:

Deletes an order with a user-provided ID from the database.

Responses:

- 200 (OK) - Order has been deleted from the database successfully.
    
- 400 (Bad Request) - ID was not numeric.
    
- 404 (Not Found) - No record was found in the database with the provided ID.

item.description: HTTP requests that interact with the "orders" table in the database are stored in this folder. Functionally they are very similar to the ones found in the Items folder.
